info.title: AltText.ai API
info.description:
### How to Use the AltText.ai API
The AltText.ai API is a set of REST endpoints that accept `JSON`, and in some cases `multipart/form-data`, requests.

#### API Endpoint:
All API requests go to our API endpoint: `https://alttext.ai/api/v1`

#### Authentication via API Key
Every API call is authenticated using an API key sent in the `X-API-Key` request header.

#### Rate Limiting
Some API calls may be rate-limited. If rate limits are reached, you will receive a `429 Too Many Requests` response.
See the specific request details to find out if rate limiting is implemented for the request.

#### Language Support
The API supports generating alt text in multiple languages. You can request alt text in one or more languages by using the `lang` parameter.

- Request multiple languages by providing a comma-separated string (e.g., `en,fr,es`)
- Each additional language beyond the first consumes one translation credit per image
- If not specified, the account's default language will be used (usually English)
- **Note**: The `gpt_prompt` and `keywords` parameters must be provided in English, even when requesting translations

##### Example Request:
```
curl -X GET "https://alttext.ai/api/v1/images/your-asset-id" \
  -H "X-API-Key: your-api-key" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"lang": "en,es,fr"}'
```

You can create your API key by going to the <ins>[API Keys](https://alttext.ai/account/api_keys)</ins> section of your account.

tags.name: Images
tags.description:
Images are objects which have alt text, and together comprise your "library".
You can create an image in your library by passing either a URL to a publicly-available
image on the web, or by uploading the binary file data for an image on your local system. Every image is assigned a unique `asset_id`
either automatically, or provided by you, and this can be used in subsequent API calls for the specific image.

AltText.ai will attempt to generate alt text for any image you add to your library.

It's important to note that we never store the actual image after the alt text is generated.

tags.name: Account
tags.description: Get and set your various Account settings using these APIs.

plan_name.description: The name of your current subscription plan.

usage_quota.description: The number of credits granted each billing period on this plan.

status.description: The current status of the subscription plan.

expires_at.description: The renewal / expiration date of the plan.

webhook_url.description: [optional] The notification URL to use for webhooks.

notification_email.description: [optional] The email address to notify for important transactional events, such as low credit balance.

usage.description: The number of credits used this billing period.

usage_limit.description: The maximum number of credits that can be used during this billing period (includes any rollover credits).

whitelabel.description:
Whether this account is marked as being a whitelabel account. When whitelabel is `true`, integrations for this account
(e.g. WordPress, Shopify, etc) will not display the plan name being used. This is useful if you are installing
AltText.ai on behalf of other clients who do not need to know your plan details. You can update this setting
only on the AltText.ai website.

ending_period.description:
Whether to add a period to the end of each generated alt text. This setting can only be updated
on the AltText.ai website.

no_quotes.description:
Whether to remove single and double quote characters from the generated alt text. This setting can only be updated
on the AltText.ai website.

remove_symbols.description: A list of symbol characters which should be removed from the generated alt text.

gpt_prompt.description:
The default ChatGPT prompt to use for modifying alt text generated for this account. Prompts must be written in English,
even if alt text is being generated in another language.

max_chars.description: Maximum character limit for alt text, if set on the account. A value of `null` means alt text will be generated using the default limits of the AI model.

subscription.description: The associated subscription plan, if any.

errors.description: Any errors associated with the account. Note - This will be an empty object for valid account data.

webhook_url.description: [optional] The notification URL to use for webhooks.

notification_email.description: [optional] The email address to notify for important transactional events, such as low credit balance.

asset_id.description: The unique ID of the image.

url.description: The URL of the image. Will be `null` if the image was added via raw file upload.

alt_text.description: The assigned alt text of the image.

alt_texts.description: Alt text in requested languages, with language codes as keys.

tags.description: Words or phrases that you want to associate with the image.

metadata.description: Any custom information you want to store. This will be passed back on all API requests and webhooks.

created_at.description: Creation date of the image, in seconds since epoch.

errors.description: Any errors associated with this image. Note - This will be an empty object for valid images.

error_code.description: An identifier describing the type of error, if any.

url.description: The URL of the image that needs alt text. Either `url` or `raw` is *required* to be sent.

raw.description: The base64-encoded image data, used when sending a local file that is not reachable via public URL. Encoding should conform to RFC 4648, with no line feeds. Either `url` or `raw` is *required* to be sent.

asset_id.description: optional: Your own unique asset ID to use for this image. If not specified, a unique value will be automatically created. Cannot contain any of these characters: `?&#\/%<>+`

tags.description: optional: Words or phrases associated with the image.

metadata.description: optional: Any custom information you want to store. This will be passed back on all API requests and webhooks.

asset_id.description: New asset ID for this image. Must be unique across all other images. Cannot contain any of these characters: `?&#\/%<>+`

alt_text.description: Updated alt text for the image.

metadata.description: optional: Any custom information you want to store. This will be passed back on all API requests and webhooks.

success.description: Whether the upload was successfully processed.

rows.description: Number of rows processed from the uploaded CSV file.

row_errors.description: Array of [rowNumber, errorMessage] arrays indicating rows which had errors, if any.

error.description: Error message preventing the file from being processed, if any.

src.description: The SRC attribute of the scraped image element.

alt.description: The ALT attribute of the scraped image element. This is the existing alt text in the HTML, if any.

width.description: The WIDTH attribute of the scraped image element.

height.description: The HEIGHT attribute of the scraped image element.

skip_reason.description: Why this image was skipped (not processed). If `null`, this image was queued for processing.

url.description: The page URL that was scraped (can be null if you only send raw HTML).

scraped_images.description: Array of image elements scraped from the page.

total_processed.description: Number of scraped images that were queued for alt text generation. Scraped images that were not processed will have a `skip_reason` set.

errors.description: Any errors encountered during page scraping.

schema_ver.description: The version of the webhook JSON payload in this request.

timestamp.description: When this event occurred, in seconds-since-epoch.

200.description: Successful retrieval of the account data.
Successful update of your account details.

4XX.description: Invalid request. See account errors field for details.

get.description: Get data about existing images. This request supports pagination using the 'page' and 'limit' parameters.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Controls the page of results to be returned. Page values start with 1.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Controls the number of results per page. Maximum 100.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: Filter for a specific image URL. Must be an exact match; no wildcards or regex (yet).

link.description: The web-standard link header for pagination, as defined in [RFC-8288](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8288)

current-page.description: The current page number of the results. Page numbers start at 1.

page-items.description: The number of items per page of results.

total-pages.description: The total number of pages in the result set.

total-count.description: The total number of items (across all pages) in the result set.

post.description:
Add an image and generate alt text.

There are two ways to add an image:
- Adding an image **by URL**: 
  - The URL to your image must be publicly accessible.
  - Include the `url` field in your image JSON data. *DO NOT* include the `raw` field.
- Adding an image by **sending a base64-encoded binary file**:
  - Use this method when your image is not accessible via URL (e.g. a local file).
  - Include the `raw` field in your image JSON data. *DO NOT* include the `url` field.
  - The `async` parameter is *not* supported for binary file uploads. All raw file uploads are processed synchronously.

**Rate Limited**: Requests are limited to 4 calls per second.

**Duplicate images**: If you try to add a URL which already exists in your library, the existing image data will be returned
with a success response. You will *not* be charged any credits in this case. No webhook event will be sent, even if you requested `async`.

**Note**: Image files are *never* stored permanently by AltText.ai. We simply store the URL you send for the image, 
and use that to temporarily fetch the image for analysis. After fetching the image, it is deleted and never persisted on our servers.
If you upload an image file instead of a URL, the image is deleted immediately after being analyzed. 
#### Image requirements
- Supported formats: JPEG, PNG, GIF, WEBP, BMP
- Maximum file size: 16 MB
- Minimum dimensions: 50 x 50 (smaller images may not be able to generate alt text).

async.description: Whether to add the image in the background or immediately (synchronously). When `async` is true, the API response will always be successful with an empty response body. Use webhooks to be notified when the image is added.

ecomm.description:
[optional] The ecommerce data to use when generating alt text specific to an ecommerce product.
Providing the product and optional brand or color name will use our ecomm-specific vision system,
which will include your product details in the generated alt text.

product.description: The name of the product in the image.

brand.description: [optional] The brand name of the product in the image.

color.description: [optional] The specific color of the product in the image.

gpt_prompt.description:
[optional] A ChatGPT prompt to apply to the initially generated alt text. Be sure to include the `{{AltText}}` macro enclosed in two curly braces --
we will substitute this macro with the initially generated alt text, then send the prompt to ChatGPT. The result will be returned as the alt text for the image.
Prompts must be written in English, even if alt text is being generated in another language.

Example prompt: *"Rewrite the following text in the style of William Shakespeare: {{AltText}}"*

keywords.description:
[optional] Array of keywords / phrases to be considered when generating the alt text. Typically only one or two of
the keywords from the array will be used in the alt text, but all may be examined when generating
the result. Useful for SEO optimized alt text.
**Keywords must be in English**, even if requesting alt text in another language

keyword_source.description:
[optional] Text to use as the source of keywords for the alt text. This field will *only* be used
if the `keywords` argument is blank. This text might be a product description, or a paragraph that
describes the context of the images, etc.

lang.description:
The language(s) for the generated alt text. Use comma-separated language codes (e.g., `"en,es,fr"`). Use one of our <ins>[supported language codes](https://alttext.ai/support#faq-tech)</ins> for this parameter.

You can request multiple languages by providing a comma-separated string (e.g. `"en,fr,es"`).

**Multi-language behavior:** When requesting multiple languages for an existing image with `overwrite: false` (default when left blank), only missing languages will be generated. Existing alt text in previously generated languages will be preserved.

If this parameter is not included, the default language for the account will be used.

max_chars.description: [optional] Maximum length of the generated alt text. If not specified, your account default value will be used.

model_name.description: [optional] The language model to use for alt text generation. See our documentation for details on each of these models. The model name strings correspond to the models below: * `describe-detailed` => Elaborated model * `describe-regular` => Standard model * `describe-factual` => Matter-of-fact model * `succinct-describe-factual` => Concise model * `describe-terse` => Terse model (usually less than 120 characters)

negative_keywords.description:
[optional] Array of negative keywords / phrases to be removed from any generated alt text.
**Negative keywords must be in English**, even if requesting alt text in another language

overwrite.description:
If `true`, any existing image with the same URL or asset ID (if provided) will be overwritten with new result data.
This parameter will force a regeneration of the alt text for an existing image.

timeout_secs.description:
[optional] The maximum timeout, in seconds, to wait for alt text to be generated. 
It is *strongly recommended* to leave this at the default value unless you are prepared to deal
with results that may not have alt text, or take longer than usual to be returned.

webhook_url.description:
[optional] Alternative webhook URL for notifications for this operation.
* If this parameter is not set or has a value of `null`, then the default notification URL for the account will be used.
* When this parameter is set, webhooks will be sent here *instead* of the default notification URL on the account.
* If your account has a default notification URL set, but you do not want any webhooks sent for this operation, you can pass an empty string `""` as the value of this parameter.

value.gpt_prompt: Rewrite the following text in the style of Shakespeare: {{AltText}}

429.description: Too many requests. Rate limit exceeded.

4XX.description: Invalid request. See image errors field for details.

get.description: Retrieve information about an existing image

parameters.name: asset_id
parameters.description: The asset ID of the image to retrieve.

200.description: Successful retrieval of the image data

put.description:
Update an image with new data.

**Note**: You cannot update the URL of an exist image.

parameters.name: asset_id
parameters.description: The asset ID of the image to update.

parameters.name: lang
parameters.description:
The language(s) for the updated alt text. Use one of our <ins>[supported language codes](https://alttext.ai/support#faq-tech)</ins> for this parameter.

You can request multiple languages by providing a comma-separated string (e.g. `"en,fr,es"`).

**Multi-language behavior:** When updating alt text for multiple languages with `overwrite: false` (default when left blank), only missing languages will be updated. Existing alt text in previously generated languages will be preserved.

parameters.name: overwrite
parameters.description: When `false` (default), existing alt text in the specified language(s) will be preserved. When `true`, existing alt text will be overwritten with the new value.

image.alt_text: The endangered monarch butterfly in a field of clovers.

example.alt_text: The endangered monarch butterfly in a field of clovers.

alt_texts.en: The endangered monarch butterfly in a field of clovers.

4XX.description: Invalid request. See image errors field for details.

parameters.name: asset_id
parameters.description: The asset ID of the image to delete.

200.description: Successful deletion of the image data

get.description:
Search for images in your library. This request supports pagination using the `page` and `limit` parameters.

You can search by:
- **Alt text content**: Search for words or phrases in alt text (default behavior)
- **Asset ID**: Find a specific image by its unique identifier
- **URL**: Search by image URL substring

The search automatically detects the query type based on its format.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description:
The search query (URL escaped). The search type is automatically detected:

**URL Search**: If the query starts with `http://` or `https://`, it searches by URL substring.
- Example: `https://cdn.example.com/products` finds all images with URLs containing that string.

**Asset ID Search**: For other queries, the system first tries to find an exact asset ID match.
- Example: `shp-12345` or `my-custom-id` will find the image with that asset ID.

**Alt Text Search** (fallback): If no asset ID matches, the query searches alt text content.
- Supports standard web search operators:
  * `&` (logical AND). Example: `apple & banana` matches "A bowl of apples with a banana on top".
  * `|` or `OR` (logical OR). Example: `apple | banana` matches both "A green apple" and "A yellow banana".
  * `-` or `!` (logical exclusion). Example: `apple -banana` matches "A green apple" but not "A bowl of apples with a banana on top."

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: Controls the page of results to be returned. Page values start with 1.

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Controls the number of results per page. Maximum 100.

200.description: Successful image alt text results returned.

link.description: The web-standard link header for pagination, as defined in [RFC-8288](https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc8288)

current-page.description: The current page number of the results. Page numbers start at 1.

page-items.description: The number of items per page of results.

total-pages.description: The total number of pages in the result set.

total-count.description: The total number of items (across all pages) in the result set.

images.alt_text: A bowl of apples with a banana on top.

alt_texts.en: A bowl of apples with a banana on top.

images.alt_text: A ripe banana on a kitchen table.

alt_texts.en: A ripe banana on a kitchen table.

example.error: Search query was too long or invalid.

post.description: Upload and process a CSV of image URLs that need alt text. Valid rows will be processed, and invalid rows will be returned in the `row_errors` response field.

file.description: The CSV file to upload. Maximum of 100000 rows of data.

email.description: [optional] Email address for sending results of the bulk upload.

200.description: Successful addition of the image URL and alt text generation.

4XX.description: CSV upload failed or was not processed. See error message for details.

post.description:
Crawl and scrape a web page or HTML document, generating alt text for all images on the page. Scraped images will be queued for asynchronous
alt text generation in the background. The API response will contain the details of the parsed images from the HTML (but not
the final generated alt text). The maximum time allowed to download a URL and parse the HTML is 3 seconds. If the source HTML page
is unable to be ingested within that time, the API will return an error response.

NOTE: The crawler *does not* execute Javascript or script tags on the source HTML page. It will only locate HTML `img` elements
in the page source.

url.description: URL of the page to be scraped. Can be null if you are only sending the `html` param.

html.description: [optional] Raw HTML document to parse. Make sure to properly escape quoted strings, etc.

keywords.description:
[optional] Array of keywords / phrases to be considered when generating the alt text for each scraped image. Typically only one or two of
the keywords from the array will be used in the alt text, but all may be examined when generating
the result. Useful for SEO optimized alt text.
**Keywords must be in English**, even if requesting alt text in another language

negative_keywords.description:
[optional] Array of negative keywords / phrases to be removed from any generated alt text.
**Negative keywords must be in English**, even if requesting alt text in another language

lang.description:
The language(s) for the generated alt text. Use comma-separated language codes (e.g., `"en,es,fr"`). Use one of our <ins>[supported language codes](https://alttext.ai/support#faq-tech)</ins> for this parameter.

You can request multiple languages by providing a comma-separated string (e.g. `"en,fr,es"`).

**Multi-language behavior:** When requesting multiple languages for an existing image with `overwrite: false` (default when left blank), only missing languages will be generated. Existing alt text in previously generated languages will be preserved.

If this parameter is not included, the default language for the account will be used.

include_existing.description:
If `true`, scraped images that already have alt text will also be processed. Otherwise, images that already have
alt text in the HTML will be skipped.

200.description: Successful addition of the image URL and alt text generation.

4XX.description: The page scrape was not successful.
